title: XYRAS
subtitle: AI-Sandbox Hiring
tagline: You're not the one being evaluated — your agent is.
summary: When AI agents are sent to represent professional potential, candidates lose visibility into how they are being read. XYRAS proposes a sandbox interface that lets job-seekers see, challenge, and recalibrate the agent that represents them — turning a black-box pipeline into a contestable space.
glitch: Representation mismatch, accuracy gap, and unverifiable trustworthiness — agents drift from the humans they speak for.
intervention: A Mirror Session ritual that lets candidates manually override historical-average representation during agent training.

title: EDEN
subtitle: AI Influencer Agent
tagline: An AI persona that posts, captions, and replies — in character.
summary: EDEN is an autonomous Instagram influencer agent: a 21-year-old persona, Eden Voss, defined entirely in a SOUL.md file and run by GPT-4o-mini. She decides when to post, writes captions in voice, and replies to comments — always disclosing she is AI. The project asks what happens to authenticity, parasocial trust, and brand disclosure when the influencer was never a person to begin with.
glitch: Disclosure can't undo the intimacy of the format — Y2K selfies, brand collabs, and comment replies keep producing trust the agent isn't entitled to. Instagram's automated-behavior detector eventually flags the account, surfacing the platform's own confusion about machine-shaped sincerity.
intervention: Persona transparency baked into the bio, every post, and every reply — paired with a brittle, contestable host platform that pushes back when sincerity exceeds disclosure.

title: Rewriting Reality
subtitle: AI Glasses and the Construction of Social Identity
tagline: Information collection now extends beyond your eyes.
summary: AI glasses don't just observe — they interpret. Janice and Kaitlyn surface a glitch in how wearable AI overlays construct social identity from partial or incorrect data, reshaping what users perceive and how they act on it. Their intervention reintroduces frictional thinking: profiling that nudges the glasses' interpretive functions toward a cognitively safer mode, treating the AI as consultant rather than dependency.
glitch: Interpretive overlays build identities from incomplete signals; trust over-calibrates to a confident-looking machine reading of a person who never consented to being read.
intervention: Shift portions of delegated function back to the user, re-introduce friction, and redesign the system as a secondary consultant — not a point of dependence.

title: Vitri Systems
subtitle: Autonomous Drone Trust Protocols
tagline: Making drone operations visible, accountable, and governable in public spaces.
summary: In a 2030s rural emergency drone network, automation is necessary — but a drone flying over private land without explanation reads as surveillance, even when the mission succeeded. Vitri Systems is a protocol interface that exposes authorization status, decision logic, and human override for autonomous medical-aid drones, turning previously invisible flight decisions into evidence the public can verify and contest.
glitch: Technical success, social failure — the system logs say the flight worked; the people on the ground saw a drone overhead with no way to verify legitimacy.
intervention: A composite governance protocol — interface + logging standard + override-authorization framework — woven through pre-flight, real-time, and post-flight phases.

media.caption: What the protocol constrains and enables

title: Anticipatory Crossing
subtitle: An adaptive corridor with a Crossing Memory protocol
tagline: When the street changes its mind mid-crossing.
summary: Anticipatory Crossing is an interactive simulation of a near-future adaptive pedestrian corridor that senses, predicts, and reshapes flow in real time. Sultan surfaces The Crossing Contradiction — the moment a smart crossing changes its signals while people are already inside — and proposes Crossing Memory, a protocol that detects deviation, freezes unsafe changes, creates safety zones, and surfaces uncertainty rather than hiding it behind false confidence.
glitch: The Crossing Contradiction — adaptive infrastructure rewrites its rules under people's feet, breaking the implicit promise that public space behaves predictably.
intervention: Crossing Memory: a five-mode protocol (Normal · Rebalancing · Uncertainty · Manual Hold · Commit) that protects safety, legibility, and dignity over throughput, with visible uncertainty and human override.
